content.problemStatement:
A perfect tree is a rooted tree such that all its leaves are at the same distance from the root. The value of a perfect tree with nnn vertices at a time xxx is
W(x)=maxp(âˆ‘i=1ndist(pi)â‹…xiâˆ’1)â€…â€Š,W(x) = \mathrm{max}_p \left(\sum_{i=1}^{n} dist(p_i) \cdot x^{i-1}\right) \;,W(x)=maxpâ€‹(âˆ‘i=1nâ€‹dist(piâ€‹)â‹…xiâˆ’1),
where the vertices of the tree are (arbitrarily) numbered 111 through nnn, the maximum is taken over all permutations ppp of integers 111 through nnn, and dist(u)dist(u)dist(u) denotes the distance of vertex uuu from the root.

Our Chef has a perfect tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN), which is rooted at vertex 111. For any vertex vvv of this tree, the value of the subtree rooted at vvv (note that it is also a perfect tree) is defined in the same way as above, where the sum and permutations are only taken over vertices of this subtree and dist()dist()dist() denotes the distance from vertex vvv.

You should answer QQQ queries. In each query, Chef has two parametres vvv and yyy and he wants to know the value of the subtree rooted at vertex vvv, at time yyy. The queries are encoded in such a way that they have to be answered online. Since the values may be large, compute them modulo 100000000710000000071000000007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7).
constraints: the graph described on the input is a perfect tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 200,000200,000200,000
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 100,000100,000100,000
1â‰¤1 \leq 1â‰¤ Sum of NNN over all test cases â‰¤10000\leq 10000â‰¤10000
1â‰¤1 \leq 1â‰¤ Sum of QQQ over all test cases â‰¤8000\leq 8000â‰¤8000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
Each of the following Nâˆ’1N-1Nâˆ’1 lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv.
The following QQQ lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers aaa and bbb. The parametres vvv and yyy are computed in the following way: let's denote the answer to the previous query by ansansans (ans=0ans = 0ans=0 for the first query); then, v=aâŠ•ansv = a \oplus ansv=aâŠ•ans and y=bâŠ•ansy = b \oplus ansy=bâŠ•ans.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer - the value of the given subtree modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: For the first query, the choice of the permutation ppp is irrelevant, the inner sum (and therefore the value of the tree) is always just the sum of distances of all vertices from vertex 111. Hence, the answer is 0+1+1+2+2+2=80+1+1+2+2+2 = 80+1+1+2+2+2=8.
In the second query, v=2v = 2v=2 and y=2y = 2y=2.
In the third query, v=3v = 3v=3 and y=3y = 3y=3.
